summary: Proposal is structurally novel (weekday gate, unused in 121 existing filters) and fully computable, but multi-value OR semantics on a single field is non-standard for the parser and the trigger reduction (~5/7) yields a weak baseline-equivalent cut.

analyses.distinctness_notes: No existing archetype slug uses `weekday` as a regime gate; 121 filters exist but none reference weekday per registry. Time-of-week is an orthogonal axis to liquidity/funding/taker-ratio dimensions already covered. Slug says `_long` but side=both — minor inconsistency, but archetype is structurally distinct.
analyses.triggers_estimate_method: Total cross-channel events ~7200/30d (1400 OI + 5000 pc + 800 R6). Weekday cut keeps 5/7 of days = ~5143. Side=both, channel=* preserves all. Estimate ~5000 triggers.
fall_through_conditions: weekday=Mon (multi-value OR not standard — parser likely AND-conjuncts these into impossible 'weekday=Mon AND weekday=Tue AND...' = zero matches, OR uses last value only)
analyses.exit_compatibility_notes: Time-regime gate is exit-agnostic. Pairs best with `fixed` (only positive-mean family in coverage: mean=0.175, n_positive=6680/7320). Avoid time_only (mean=-0.73) and multi_tp (mean=-0.28). Caveat: filter is so loose it nearly equals channel=*/side=both baseline, so exit choice will dominate the score signal.
